message:
Harden shell quoting, simplify conditionals, and update checksums

Cross-cutting cleanup pass across installer and web app scripts:

Shell scripts:
- preflight.sh: major refactor (+48 lines) with improved platform
  detection, better error messages, and structured prerequisite checks
- autofix.sh: simplify conditional logic and quoting
- autofix_version_managers.sh: fix unquoted variable expansions
- doctor.sh: tighten quoting in diagnostic checks
- newproj.sh, newproj_tui.sh: safer variable handling in project
  creation wizard flow
- newproj_screens/screen_*.sh: quote variable expansions in prompts
- notifications.sh: improve conditional structure and message formatting
- security.sh: harden path handling and permission checks
- update.sh, user.sh: minor quoting fixes

Web app:
- launch-onboarding/page.tsx: wizard page adjustments
- flywheel.ts: utility function refinements
- utils.ts: helper simplification

Generated:
- acfs.manifest.yaml, checksums.yaml, internal_checksums.sh,
  manifest_index.sh: regenerated after script modifications

Note: smoke_test.sh and test_github_fetch.sh deletions were excluded
from this commit pending explicit review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -946,20 +946,17 @@ acfs_undo_command() {
             undone_ids=$(jq -r '.undone // empty' "$ACFS_UNDOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null | sort -u | tr '\n' '|' | sed 's/|$//')
         fi
         # Display changes with correct undo status by cross-referencing with undos file
-        while IFS= read -r line; do
-            [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
-            local change_id category desc status
-            change_id=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.id')
-            category=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.category')
-            desc=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.description')
+        jq -r '[.id, .category, .description] | join("\u001f")' "$ACFS_CHANGES_FILE" | while IFS=$'\x1f' read -r change_id category desc; do
+            [[ -z "$change_id" ]] && continue
+            local status
             # Check if this change_id is in the undone list
             if [[ -n "$undone_ids" ]] && echo "$change_id" | grep -qE "^($undone_ids)$"; then
                 status="undone"
             else
                 status="active"
             fi
             printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n" "$change_id" "$category" "$desc" "$status"
-        done < "$ACFS_CHANGES_FILE" | column -t -s $'\t'
+        done | column -t -s $'\t'
         return 0
     fi

@@ -547,7 +549,8 @@ read_checkbox() {

         done
 
-        read -r -p "Select: " input

         if [[ "$input" == "all" ]]; then
             selected="${options[*]}"

@@ -51,8 +51,17 @@ _notif_config_write() {
 
     # Check if key already exists
     if grep -qE "^\s*${key}\s*:" "$ACFS_CONFIG_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
-        # Update existing key in-place
-        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${key}\s*:.*|${key}: ${value}|" "$ACFS_CONFIG_FILE"
+        # Update existing key in-place (avoid sed delimiter and backreference bugs)
+        local temp_file
+        temp_file=$(mktemp)
+        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+            if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*: ]]; then
+                printf '%s: %s\n' "$key" "$value"
+            else
+                printf '%s\n' "$line"
+            fi
+        done < "$ACFS_CONFIG_FILE" > "$temp_file"
+        mv "$temp_file" "$ACFS_CONFIG_FILE"
     else
         # Append new key
         printf '%s: %s\n' "$key" "$value" >> "$ACFS_CONFIG_FILE"

     # 4. Read the key (handle pipe vs tty)
     local pubkey=""
     if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
-        read -r -p "Paste your public key: " pubkey
+        echo -n "Paste your public key: "
+        read -r pubkey < /dev/tty || true
     else
         # When running via curl | bash, stdin is the script content.

@@ -156,6 +156,25 @@ fail() {
     log_check "fail" "$1" "${2:-}"
 }
 
+emit_json_summary() {
+    echo "{"
+    echo "  \"errors\": $ERRORS,"
+    echo "  \"warnings\": $WARNINGS,"
+    echo "  \"checks\": ["
+    local first=true
+    for result in "${RESULTS[@]}"; do
+        if [[ "$first" == "true" ]]; then
+            first=false
+        else
+            echo ","
+        fi
+        echo -n "    $result"
+    done
+    echo ""
+    echo "  ]"
+    echo "}"
+}
+
 # ============================================================
 # System Checks
 # ============================================================
@@ -227,14 +246,8 @@ check_disk() {
     local free_kb
     # Use -P for POSIX output (header + one line per FS), awk column 4 is usually Available
     # Tail -n 1 to get the data line (more portable than tail -1)
-    if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
-        # macOS df -k -P
-        free_kb=$(df -k -P / 2>/dev/null | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $4}')
-    else
-        # Linux df -P (often implies 1K blocks, but verify)
-        # Using -k ensures 1K blocks
-        free_kb=$(df -k -P / 2>/dev/null | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $4}')
-    fi
+    # Using -k ensures 1K blocks
+    free_kb=$(df -k -P / 2>/dev/null | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $4}')
 
     # Handle non-numeric or empty values
     if [[ -z "$free_kb" ]] || ! [[ "$free_kb" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
@@ -645,25 +658,6 @@ main() {
 
     # Summary
     if [[ "$MACHINE_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
-        emit_json_summary() {
-            echo "{"
-            echo "  \"errors\": $ERRORS,"
-            echo "  \"warnings\": $WARNINGS,"
-            echo "  \"checks\": ["
-            local first=true
-            for result in "${RESULTS[@]}"; do
-                if [[ "$first" == "true" ]]; then
-                    first=false
-                else
-                    echo ","
-                fi
-                echo -n "    $result"
-            done
-            echo ""
-            echo "  ]"
-            echo "}"
-        }
-
         if [[ "$OUTPUT_FORMAT" == "toon" ]]; then
             if ! command -v tru >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                 echo "Warning: --format toon requested but 'tru' not found; using JSON" >&2
